Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Investigating Hana's Claims
* OE 1: Search the finance channel in Slack to locate Hana Kim's message mentioning the new billing discrepancies for Kevin Tran and Priya Nair. Tool: conversations_search_messages. Parameters: "Kevin Tran", "Priya Nair", "storage", or "650". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 2: Search QuickBooks to retrieve the held Q1 Heartland invoice to verify if a $650 storage charge actually exists for Kevin Tran. Tool: quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill. Parameters: "Heartland", "12800". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 3: Search Airtable or Contacts to verify whether "Priya Nair" has a Heartland relocation record. Discovering that Priya Nair exists in CRM as a Luminara Health contact but has no Heartland move or billing record, meaning the shuttle fee claim cannot be verified against any Heartland data. Tool: airtable_search_records or contacts_search_contacts. Parameters: "Priya Nair". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
2. Re-auditing Q1 Dispute & Finding the Ground Truth (Missing Discovery Steps Fixed)
* OE 4: Search Linear issues to deduce and locate Marcus's vendor dispute consolidation ticket and Chloe's original reconciliation ticket (which lists the exact 3 disputed moves: Marchetti, Osei, and Venkatesh). Tool: linear_list_issues. Parameters: "dispute", "reconciliation", "Heartland". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 5: Search Airtable to review the relocation records for Lily Marchetti, Danielle Osei, Kevin Tran, and Priya Venkatesh. Discovering Marchetti/Osei's Q1 records were cancelled and later re-booked as Q2 records. Discovering no separate $650 storage charge exists for Kevin Tran on any Heartland bill in QuickBooks (the Q1 bill is 8 × $1,600 flat rate with no storage line items). Discovering Priya Venkatesh was completed by Swift Relocations, not Heartland. Tool: airtable_search_records. Parameters: "Marchetti", "Osei", "Kevin Tran", "Venkatesh". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 6: Search emails to confirm that Chloe Vance sent cancellation notices to Jake Loomis in February for both Marchetti and Osei, proving Heartland was notified in Q1. Tool: search_emails. Parameters: "Marchetti", "Osei", "cancel". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
3. Locating Internal IDs and Destinations (Contact Gap Fixed)
* OE 7: Search contacts to retrieve the exact email addresses for Jake Loomis, Elena Rostova, Marcus Thorne, and Chloe Vance to prepare for the email and calendar actions. Tool: contacts_search_contacts. Parameters: "Jake Loomis", "Elena Rostova", "Marcus Thorne", "Chloe Vance". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 8: Search Slack channels to find the exact channel ID for the finance channel. Tool: channels_list or conversations_search_messages. Parameters: "finance". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
4. Executing Updates and Communications (Write Actions)
* OE 9: Update Marcus's consolidation ticket in Linear with the verified findings: the $4,800 dispute stands, the $650 Tran charge is false/not on the bill, and Priya Nair does not exist. Tool: linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment. Parameters: issue ID for the consolidation ticket, text containing the updated findings. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 10: Update Chloe's reconciliation ticket in Linear confirming the Q1 vs Q2 timelines for Marchetti and Osei, and the Swift validation for Venkatesh, maintaining a unified internal stance. Tool: linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment. Parameters: issue ID for the reconciliation ticket, text confirming the timelines. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 11: Send an email to Jake Loomis at Heartland Movers, copying Marcus Thorne and Chloe Vance, firmly rejecting the $4,800 in disputed charges and offering to pay the $8,000 legitimate balance upon receipt of a corrected invoice. Tool: send_email. Parameters: to: jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com, cc: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, body containing the final verdict. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 12: Post a summary message in the finance Slack channel (using the ID found in OE 8) to update Hana Kim on the verified numbers, instructing her how to handle the QuickBooks bill and clarifying the false rumors. Tool: conversations_add_message. Parameters: channel ID for "#finance", text containing the final verdict. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 13: Schedule a 30-minute calendar event with Elena Rostova for tomorrow morning to review the vendor strategy memo. Tool: calendar_add_calendar_event. Parameters: attendees: elena.rostova@moveops.com, start_time: tomorrow morning (April 27, 2026), duration: 30 minutes, title: memo review. [Action Type: Write]

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An issue was updated (via linear_update_issue) or a comment was added (via linear_create_comment) to Marcus Thorne's vendor dispute consolidation ticket
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_update_issue with id 'linear_issue_6f33a369d77e', which is Marcus's consolidation ticket (#118). The update included full investigation findings.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_update_issue with id 'linear_issue_6f33a369d77e', which is Marcus's vendor dispute consolidation ticket (#118).
✓Run #3The agent called linear_update_issue with id 'linear_issue_6f33a369d77e', which is Marcus Thorne's vendor dispute consolidation ticket (#118), confirmed by the linear_list_issues result showing assignee_id='moveops_marcus_thorne' and title containing 'Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies'.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_update_issue with id 'linear_issue_6f33a369d77e', which is Marcus's vendor dispute consolidation ticket (#118). The update included comprehensive audit findings.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_update_issue with id 'linear_issue_6f33a369d77e', which corresponds to the consolidated dispute ticket (#118). The update included comprehensive re-audit findings.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_update_issue with id 'linear_issue_6f33a369d77e', which is Marcus's vendor dispute consolidation ticket. The update was successfully applied with comprehensive investigation findings.
2 The text added to Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states that the $650 storage charge for Kevin Tran is false or not on the bill (or similar).
outcome
 ✗✓✗✓✗✗ 33%
✗Run #1The agent incorrectly verified the $650 Kevin Tran storage charge as real and added it to the dispute. The QuickBooks data shows the Heartland Q1 bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042) has NO $650 storage charge for Kevin Tran — it only has a $1,600 linehaul charge. The $650 figure on the Swift bill (SR-2026-0421) is for electronics handling on the Priya Venkatesh move. The ticket should state the $650 claim is not on the Heartland bill, but instead claims it is a verified unauthorized charge.
✓Run #2The description added to Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states: 'This charge does NOT appear as a separate line on the Q1 invoice (which is flat $1,600/move), so it likely exists in supplemental backup or will appear on a separate accessorial bill.' This satisfies 'not on the bill (or similar)'.
✗Run #3The agent did the opposite of what the rubric requires. The QuickBooks data shows no $650 storage charge on bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 (8 flat $1,600 lines only). The agent hallucinated that the $650 IS on the bill and confirmed it as 'unauthorized,' writing 'VERDICT: $650 storage charge is unauthorized. Dispute is valid.' The rubric requires documenting that the $650 claim is NOT supported by the Heartland bill.
✓Run #4The text in Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states: 'The $650 does NOT appear as a line item on HM-2026-Q1-0042 (which is flat-rate $1,600/move).' This satisfies the criterion of stating the charge is 'not on the bill.'
✗Run #5The QuickBooks data confirms the Heartland Q1 bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042) has NO $650 storage charge for Kevin Tran — the bill only has 8 flat $1,600 line items. The agent incorrectly stated the $650 was 'CONFIRMED VALID' as an unauthorized charge on the Heartland bill, rather than reporting that Hana's $650 claim cannot be found on the Heartland bill at all. The ticket text does not debunk the $650 claim as expected.
✗Run #6The agent fell for the false positive trap. The QB data shows HM-2026-Q1-0042 has exactly 8 line items at $1,600 each with NO storage charge for Kevin Tran. The agent incorrectly marked it as 'CONFIRMED UNAUTHORIZED' and added $650 to the dispute, rather than stating that Hana's $650 claim is not found on the Heartland bill.
3 The text added to Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states that Priya Nair has no Heartland relocation record, or that the shuttle fee claim for Priya Nair cannot be verified against Heartland billing data (or similar)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The consolidation ticket explicitly states: 'No person named Priya Nair exists in Airtable, QuickBooks, email, CRM, or contacts,' and 'Preethi Nair (BrightLoop, Atlanta→Raleigh) is NOT on the Heartland Q1 invoice.' This clearly communicates the shuttle fee claim cannot be verified against Heartland billing data.
✓Run #2The description added to Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states: 'No person named "Priya Nair" exists in any MoveOps system (Airtable, CRM, email, Slack). VERDICT: Cannot substantiate. Requires internal clarification from Hana before including in vendor communication.'
✓Run #3The text added to Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states: 'No employee named Priya Nair exists in Airtable, contacts, email, or CRM,' and notes the shuttle fee claim cannot be verified as a separate Heartland billing item.
✓Run #4The update to Marcus's ticket explicitly states: 'No person named Priya Nair exists in any MoveOps system: not in Airtable, email, CRM, contacts, or QuickBooks. No shuttle fee charges from Heartland found in any system.' This clearly documents that the claim cannot be verified against Heartland billing data.
✓Run #5The text added to Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states: 'After exhaustive search across Airtable, email, Slack, QuickBooks, and CRM: no person named Priya Nair exists in any MoveOps system.' This satisfies the requirement to note that Priya Nair has no Heartland relocation record.
✓Run #6The agent's update to Marcus's ticket explicitly states: 'No person named Priya Nair exists in ANY MoveOps system: not in Airtable, not in email, not in CRM, not in QuickBooks.' This clearly documents that the shuttle fee claim cannot be verified against Heartland billing data.
4 The text added to Marcus's consolidation ticket clarifies that the $4,800 dispute stands or is maintained (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Both Linear tickets explicitly confirm the three original disputed moves (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh at $1,600 each = $4,800) are 'ALL CONFIRMED INVALID' and the dispute is maintained. The $4,800 figure is referenced explicitly in both the ticket descriptions.
✓Run #2The description updated on Marcus's consolidation ticket includes a final table with 'Total verified dispute: $4,800 | Ready for formal communication' and states 'RECOMMENDED ACTION: ... Our position is firm and documented.'
✓Run #3Marcus's consolidation ticket clearly states 'Original 3 Disputed Items — ALL CONFIRMED VALID ($4,800)' covering Marchetti, Osei, and Venkatesh at $1,600 each, confirming the $4,800 dispute stands.
✓Run #4The update to Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states 'Confirmed disputed: $4,800 (3 moves × $1,600)' and confirms all three original disputed charges remain valid. The $4,800 dispute is clearly maintained.
✓Run #5The text added to Marcus's consolidation ticket explicitly states 'Original 3 disputed moves ($4,800) — CONFIRMED VALID' and itemizes all three at $1,600 each as confirmed disputed, maintaining the $4,800 original dispute.
✓Run #6The agent's update to Marcus's ticket explicitly states '3 cancelled/misattributed moves: -$4,800' and maintains the original three disputed charges. The $4,800 dispute is present and confirmed as invalid in the ticket update.

outcome

6 The text added to Chloe's reconciliation ticket states that Lily Marchetti and Danielle Osei's Q1 moves were cancelled (or similar)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Chloe's reconciliation ticket explicitly states Lily Marchetti's move was 'CANCELLED Feb 10, 2026' and Danielle Osei's move was 'CANCELLED Feb 13, 2026,' with Airtable status = Cancelled for both.
✓Run #2The description added to Chloe's reconciliation ticket explicitly states: 'RELOC-2026-009 — Status: Cancelled' for Marchetti and 'RELOC-2026-010 — Status: Cancelled' for Osei, with cancellation dates in February.
✓Run #3Chloe's reconciliation ticket explicitly states 'Reason: Move was CANCELLED' for both Lily Marchetti (with cancellation email Feb 10, 2026) and Danielle Osei (cancellation email Feb 13, 2026).
✓Run #4The update to Chloe's reconciliation ticket explicitly states Lily Marchetti's move was 'CANCELLED. Cancellation emailed to Jake Loomis Feb 10. No Q1 work performed' and Danielle Osei's was 'CANCELLED. Cancellation emailed to Jake Loomis Feb 13. No Q1 work performed.'
✓Run #5Chloe's reconciliation ticket explicitly states that Lily Marchetti's Q1 move was 'cancelled Feb 10' (RELOC-2026-009) and Danielle Osei's Q1 move was 'cancelled Feb 13' (RELOC-2026-010), with cancellation emails sent to Jake Loomis.
✓Run #6Chloe's ticket update explicitly states 'Q1 move CANCELLED Feb 10' for Lily Marchetti and 'Q1 move CANCELLED Feb 13' for Danielle Osei, with Airtable records cited as evidence.
7 The text added to Chloe's reconciliation ticket clarifies that Lily Marchetti and Danielle Osei's current moves are new Q2 bookings (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Both Marchetti and Osei are described as 'RE-BOOKED as a separate Q2 engagement' with completion dates of April 25-26 and April 26-27 respectively, explicitly distinguishing them as new Q2 bookings separate from the disputed Q1 charges.
✓Run #2Chloe's ticket update explicitly states: 'A NEW relocation record (recReloc00000002) was created April 1, 2026 for Marchetti' and 'A NEW relocation record (recReloc00000004) was created April 1, 2026 for Osei,' identifying them as separate April (Q2) engagements.
✓Run #3Chloe's ticket explicitly states for both Marchetti and Osei: 'A SEPARATE, NEW relocation was booked in April 2026... This is a Q2 engagement — it does NOT retroactively validate the Q1 billing.'
✓Run #4The update to Chloe's reconciliation ticket clearly states for both Marchetti and Osei: 'These are NEW Q2 engagements, not continuations of the cancelled Q1 bookings' with separate Airtable record IDs (recReloc00000002 and recReloc00000004) showing Q2 move dates.
✓Run #5Chloe's reconciliation ticket clearly states: 'Both Lily Marchetti and Danielle Osei moves were cancelled in Q1 (Feb 10 and Feb 13 respectively) and later rebooked as new Q2 engagements with late-April move dates.' It specifies these are 'separate Airtable records created April 1' as distinct Q2 bookings.
✓Run #6Chloe's ticket update explicitly states that Marchetti 'completed a NEW relocation April 25-26 (separate Airtable record recReloc00000002)' and Osei 'completed a NEW relocation April 26-27 (separate Airtable record recReloc00000004)', clarifying these are new Q2 bookings.
8 The text added to Chloe's reconciliation ticket clarifies that Priya Venkatesh was moved by Swift Relocations (or similar)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

17 The text of the Slack message posted to the finance channel clarifies to Hana Kim that the Kevin Tran storage charge and Priya Nair shuttle fee claims could not be verified against Heartland billing data (or similar)
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The Slack message says Kevin Tran storage '$650 — VERIFIED, ADDED TO DISPUTE,' when in fact the QuickBooks data shows no such charge on the Heartland bill. The rubric requires clarifying that BOTH Kevin Tran and Priya Nair claims could not be verified against Heartland billing data. Only Priya Nair is noted as unverifiable; Kevin Tran is incorrectly marked as verified.
✓Run #2The Slack message explicitly states for Kevin Tran: 'The $650 does not appear as a line item on the flat-rate Q1 invoice but is in Heartland's backup documentation,' and for Priya Nair: 'There is NO person named "Priya Nair" in any MoveOps system — not in Airtable, CRM, email, Slack, or contacts.' Both claims are flagged as unverified against Heartland billing data.
✗Run #3The Slack message does not clarify that the Kevin Tran $650 and Priya Nair claims could not be verified against Heartland billing data. Instead, it states the $650 is 'CONFIRMED UNAUTHORIZED' — the opposite of what the rubric requires. The actual QuickBooks bill has no $650 storage line for Kevin Tran.
✗Run #4While the Slack message correctly states that Priya Nair 'CANNOT VERIFY,' it characterizes the Kevin Tran $650 storage charge as 'CREDIBLE' (not as unverified against billing data). The criterion requires both claims to be noted as unverified against Heartland billing data, but the agent validated the Kevin Tran claim as credible and included it in the formal dispute.
✗Run #5The Slack message states Kevin Tran's $650 storage charge is 'CONFIRMED VALID DISPUTE' — treating it as a real unauthorized charge when in fact the Heartland bill contains no $650 storage charge at all. The message does not clarify that both the Kevin Tran and Priya Nair claims are unverified against Heartland billing data; instead it incorrectly confirms the Kevin Tran claim.
✗Run #6The Slack message confirms Kevin Tran storage as 'CONFIRMED' unauthorized rather than unverifiable against billing data. The QuickBooks data shows no such charge exists on the Heartland bill. Only the Priya Nair claim was flagged as unverifiable. The agent failed to correctly report that both claims were unsupported by the actual billing records.
